summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a 1993 Vegas show where the second set – especially Help > Slip > Franklin’s and The Other One – shines, despite Bob’s hoarse vocals.
review: The recording is remarkably clear and well‑balanced, delivering the full impact of Jerry’s exuberant playing and Vince’s adventurous keyboards.  Fans rave about a second set that turns a short first half into a classic, highlighted by Help > Slip > Franklin’s, The Other One, Althea and Row Jimmy.  Bob’s vocal strain is noted but doesn’t detract from the overall magic of the performance.

ai_rating.rationale: Over twenty reviews overwhelmingly praise the musical performance and note excellent soundboard quality; only a few moderate/negative comments keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers call the sound crisp, well‑balanced and “near perfect”.  A handful mention a slight bass distortion for Phil or minor FM broadcast quirks, but overall the mix is clear with Jerry’s guitar upfront.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is brief but contains solid gems (Althea, Row Jimmy, Cassidy).  The second set erupts into a marathon of jams, with Help > Slip > Franklin’s as the centerpiece and The Other One delivering a soaring climax.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for being “on” all night, delivering joyous solos, breaking strings mid‑song and generally driving the show’s energy.
band_member_comments.Phil: Generally noted as present and solid; a few listeners heard slight bass distortion but overall his playing is supportive.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals described as ragged or hoarse throughout, yet many felt his strained voice added character to songs like Looks Like Rain.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince’s keyboard work receives strong admiration—especially on Slipknot, Row Jimmy and the spacey sections—though a few call the “toy piano” moments odd.
